title: Fatigue and Pallor - Iron Deficiency Anemia
chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue for 6 months, pale skin, shortness of breath on exertion, and brittle nails. Heavy menstrual periods.

physical_exam.general: Pale conjunctivae and nail beds, koilonychia, angular cheilitis, smooth tongue

hidden_data.cbc: Hb: 7.5 g/dL, MCV: 68 fL (microcytic), MCH: 22 pg, RDW: 18% (high)
hidden_data.iron_studies: Ferritin: 4 ng/mL (very low), Iron: 20 mcg/dL, TIBC: 480 mcg/dL, Transferrin saturation: 4%

key_learning_points: Microcytic + high RDW = iron deficiency until proven

patient_responses.fatigue: I'm exhausted, can barely climb stairs
patient_responses.menses: My periods are very heavy, 8-9 days with clots

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Severe iron deficiency from menorrhagia.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is iron deficiency anemia. The microcytosis + high RDW + low ferritin confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Iron deficiency: Ferrous sulfate 325mg TID (65mg elemental iron). Take with vitamin C for absorption. Expect Hb rise of 1g/dL every 2-3 weeks. Continue iron for 3-6 months after Hb normalizes. Address menorrhagia with gynecology.
